title: Build a correlation matrix with any psych::fa(cor=...) method
summary: Compute a variable × variable correlation (or covariance) matrix and write it as both a CSV (long-compatible header) and a three-sheet xlsx (correlation, n_obs, meta). Intended as the matrix feeding a downstream factor-analysis fa run, or as a standalone diagnostic.
description: Loads the chosen subset of columns, optionally consulting `meta` to resolve per-column types for `mixed`/biserial/polyserial dispatch, and computes pairwise correlations using the selected method. The CSV output has a leading blank header cell, variable names as the first column, and standard numeric formatting; the xlsx has a `correlation` sheet with bold headers and 0.0000 numeric format, an `n_obs` sheet reporting the pairwise sample sizes, and a `meta` sheet recording the method and variable count.
usage:
[["factor-analysis::correlation"]]
input = "./data.csv"
run_dir = "./runs"
run_name = "cor1"
method = "pearson"
# optional:
meta = "./meta.csv"
vars = ["q1", "q2", "q3"]

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run; no '..', '/' or '\\'.

arguments.name: method
arguments.description: pearson|covariance|tetrachoric|polychoric|mixed|biserial|polyserial|yuleb|yuleq|yuley. All methods implemented, matching psych::fa(cor=...) conventions.

arguments.name: meta
arguments.description: Optional variable metadata CSV. Required for `mixed`/biserial/polyserial to determine per-column types.

arguments.name: vars
arguments.description: Subset of input columns to include. Empty = all columns.

arguments.name: correct
arguments.description: Continuity correction: replace zero cells in contingency tables used by tetrachoric/polychoric/yule. Matches psych defaults.

arguments.name: na_action
arguments.description: Pairwise deletion or abort on any NA.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether CSV inputs have a header row.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Number of worker threads for per-pair correlation parallelism. `1` (default) runs serially. Results are deterministic regardless of thread count.

arguments.name: output_format
arguments.description: Override stdout format: human, protocol, or quiet.

notes: The `covariance` method produces the unnormalised sample covariance (divisor n-1), not a correlation.
Pairwise sample sizes are written to the `n_obs` sheet so downstream consumers can weight the matrix appropriately.

artifacts.title: Correlation matrix (flat CSV)
artifacts.description: The correlation matrix in its simplest form — one row and one column per variable, ready to open in Excel, paste into R/Python, or visualise with any correlogram tool.

fields.name: (header cell, row 1 column 1)
fields.description: Blank — the top-left corner. Variable names run along the first row and the first column.

fields.name: first column
fields.description: Variable names, in the same order as the columns.

fields.name: remaining cells
fields.description: The full correlation matrix (both upper and lower triangles written — the matrix is symmetric). Values near ±1 indicate strong relationships; near 0, no linear relationship.

artifacts.title: Correlation workbook (three sheets)
artifacts.description: The same matrix plus the extra context an Excel-using reviewer typically wants: sample sizes per cell and the run metadata.

artifacts.title: Correlations with formatted headers
artifacts.description: The same matrix as the CSV, but with bold headers and a four-decimal number format for easier reading on screen.

fields.name: remaining columns
fields.description: Pairwise correlations (or covariances if method is `covariance`).

artifacts.title: Pairwise sample sizes
artifacts.description: A cell-for-cell twin of the correlation sheet showing how many respondents contributed to each pair. Rows/columns where n_obs drops sharply signal items with heavy missingness — worth investigating before trusting the correlations involving them.

fields.name: remaining cells
fields.description: Number of respondents with non-missing values on both the row and the column item.

artifacts.title: Run metadata
artifacts.description: A small key/value block recording the settings used, so a colleague opening the workbook can see what was run without re-reading the task file.

fields.name: method
fields.description: Correlation method used for this run (e.g. `pearson`, `polychoric`).

fields.name: n_vars
fields.description: Number of variables included in the matrix.
